Name: APlayerState
Description:
A PlayerState is created for every player on a server (or in a standalone game).
  PlayerStates are replicated to all clients, and contain network game relevant information about the player, such as playername, score, etc.

Variables.Name: Ping
Variables.Description: Replicated compressed ping for this player (holds ping in msec divided by 4)

Variables.Name: PlayerName
Variables.Description: Player name, or blank if none.

Variables.Name: PlayerId
Variables.Description: Unique net id number. Actual value varies based on current online subsystem, use it only as a guaranteed unique number per player.

Variables.Name: bIsSpectator
Variables.Description: Whether this player is currently a spectator

Variables.Name: bOnlySpectator
Variables.Description: Whether this player can only ever be a spectator

Variables.Name: bIsABot
Variables.Description: True if this PlayerState is associated with an AIController

Variables.Name: bIsInactive
Variables.Description: Means this PlayerState came from the GameMode's InactivePlayerArray

Variables.Name: bFromPreviousLevel
Variables.Description:
indicates this is a PlayerState from the previous level of a seamless travel,
	  waiting for the player to finish the transition before creating a new one
	  this is used to avoid preserving the PlayerState in the InactivePlayerArray if the player leaves

Variables.Name: StartTime
Variables.Description: Elapsed time on server when this PlayerState was first created.

Variables.Name: EngineMessageClass
Variables.Description: This is used for sending game agnostic messages that can be localized

Variables.Name: SavedNetworkAddress
Variables.Description: Used to match up InactivePlayerState with rejoining playercontroller.

Variables.Name: UniqueId
Variables.Description:
The id used by the network to uniquely identify a player.
	  NOTE: the internals of this property should never be exposed to the player as it's transient
	  and opaque in meaning (ie it might mean datetime followed by something else).
	  It is OK to use and pass around this property, though.

Functions.Name: ReceiveOverrideWith
Functions.Description: Can be implemented in Blueprint Child to move more properties from old to new PlayerState when reconnecting

Params.Name: OldPlayerState
Params.Description: Old PlayerState, which we use to fill the new one with

Functions.Name: ReceiveCopyProperties
Functions.Description: Can be implemented in Blueprint Child to move more properties from old to new PlayerState when traveling to a new level

Params.Name: NewPlayerState
Params.Description: New PlayerState, which we fill with the current properties
